categories.name: மாரடைப்பு
categories.description: A heart attack occurs when blood flow to the heart is blocked. Immediate action is critical.
immediateSteps: Call 108 or local emergency number immediately
Have the person sit down and rest in a comfortable position
Loosen any tight clothing around chest and neck
If the person has prescribed nitroglycerin, help them take it
Give aspirin (325mg) if not allergic and conscious
Monitor breathing and consciousness continuously
Be prepared to perform CPR if person becomes unresponsive
doList: Call emergency services immediately
Keep the person calm and still
Loosen tight clothing
Give aspirin if not allergic
Monitor vital signs
Note the time symptoms started
dontList: Do NOT let the person walk or exert themselves
Do NOT ignore symptoms even if they seem mild
Do NOT give water if person is unconscious
Do NOT delay calling emergency services
Do NOT leave the person alone
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 IMMEDIATELY. Every minute counts during a heart attack.
preventionTips: Maintain a healthy diet low in saturated fats
Exercise regularly for at least 30 minutes daily

categories.name: பாம்பு கடி
categories.description: Snake bites can be venomous and life-threatening. Quick, correct response is essential.
immediateSteps: Move away from the snake to prevent additional bites
Call 108 or rush to nearest hospital immediately
Keep the person calm and still to slow venom spread
Remove jewelry and tight clothing near the bite
Keep the bitten limb immobilized and below heart level
Mark the edge of swelling with a pen and note the time
Try to remember the snake appearance for identification
doList: Keep the victim calm and immobile
Immobilize the bitten limb with a splint
Remove rings, watches, tight clothing
Get to a hospital with anti-venom as fast as possible
Note the time of the bite
Take a photo of the snake if safe to do so
dontList: Do NOT cut the wound or try to suck out venom
Do NOT apply a tourniquet
Do NOT apply ice or cold packs
Do NOT give the person alcohol or caffeine
Do NOT try to catch or kill the snake
Do NOT apply electric shock
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 immediately for ALL snake bites. Treat every bite as potentially venomous.
preventionTips: Wear boots and long pants in snake-prone areas
Use a flashlight when walking at night
Never reach into holes or under rocks
Keep surroundings clean to avoid attracting rodents

categories.name: மூச்சுத் திணறல்
categories.description: Choking occurs when an object blocks the airway. Immediate action can save a life.
immediateSteps: Ask the person: Can you cough? Can you speak?
If they can cough forcefully, encourage continued coughing
If they cannot breathe or speak, stand behind them
Place your fist above their navel, thumb side in
Grasp fist with other hand and thrust inward and upward
Repeat abdominal thrusts until object is expelled
If person becomes unconscious, begin CPR and call 108
doList: Encourage coughing if person can still breathe partially
Perform Heimlich maneuver (abdominal thrusts) for adults
For infants: 5 back blows followed by 5 chest thrusts
Call 108 if object is not expelled quickly
Begin CPR if person becomes unconscious
dontList: Do NOT perform blind finger sweeps
Do NOT slap the back of a conscious adult (use Heimlich)
Do NOT give water to try to wash the object down
Do NOT panic - act quickly and calmly
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 if the person cannot breathe, speak, or cough, or if they become unconscious.
preventionTips: Chew food thoroughly and eat slowly
Keep small objects away from children
Cut food into small pieces for young children
Avoid talking or laughing while eating

categories.name: Severe Bleeding
categories.description: Severe bleeding can be life-threatening within minutes. Immediate pressure is critical.
immediateSteps: Call 108 for severe or uncontrollable bleeding
Apply direct, firm pressure with a clean cloth or bandage
Do not remove the cloth if blood soaks through - add more layers
Elevate the injured area above heart level if possible
If bleeding is from a limb, apply a tourniquet as last resort
Keep the person lying down and warm to prevent shock
Monitor consciousness and breathing
doList: Apply direct pressure immediately
Use clean cloth, gauze, or clothing as a bandage
Elevate the wound above heart level
Keep the person warm and calm
Apply pressure to pressure points if needed
dontList: Do NOT remove embedded objects
Do NOT remove blood-soaked bandages - add more on top
Do NOT apply a tourniquet unless absolutely necessary
Do NOT give food or water if surgery may be needed
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 for any bleeding that does not stop after 10 minutes of pressure, or if blood is spurting.
preventionTips: Keep a first aid kit accessible
Wear protective gear during risky activities

categories.name: தீக்காயங்கள்
categories.description: Burns range from minor to life-threatening. Proper first aid prevents further damage.
immediateSteps: Remove the person from the source of the burn
Cool the burn under cool (not cold) running water for 10-20 minutes
Remove clothing and jewelry near the burn (unless stuck)
Cover the burn loosely with a sterile, non-stick bandage
For chemical burns, flush with water for at least 20 minutes
Do not apply ice, butter, toothpaste, or home remedies
Seek medical help for burns larger than your palm
doList: Cool with running water for 10-20 minutes
Cover loosely with sterile bandage
Take over-the-counter pain relief if needed
Keep the person hydrated
Watch for signs of shock
dontList: Do NOT apply ice directly to the burn
Do NOT apply butter, oil, or toothpaste
Do NOT break blisters
Do NOT remove clothing stuck to the burn
Do NOT use fluffy cotton on the burn
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 for burns covering large areas, burns on face/hands/joints, chemical or electrical burns, or if the person shows signs of shock.
preventionTips: Keep hot liquids away from children

categories.name: Fractures
categories.description: A fracture is a broken bone. Proper immobilization prevents further injury.
immediateSteps: Keep the person still - do not move the injured area
Call 108 for open fractures or suspected spine/neck injury
Immobilize the injured area using a splint or padding
Apply ice wrapped in cloth to reduce swelling (20 min on/off)
Elevate the injured limb if possible
Control any bleeding with gentle pressure
Watch for signs of shock
doList: Immobilize the area above and below the fracture
Apply ice wrapped in cloth
Keep the person comfortable and warm
Support the injury with pillows or padding
Get medical imaging to confirm fracture type
dontList: Do NOT try to straighten or realign the bone
Do NOT move the person if spine injury is suspected
Do NOT apply ice directly to skin
Do NOT let the person bear weight on the injury
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 for open fractures, suspected spine/neck injuries, or if the person cannot move.
preventionTips: Ensure adequate calcium and vitamin D intake
Use proper safety equipment during sports

categories.name: மூழ்குதல்
categories.description: Drowning can cause death in minutes. Every second counts for rescue and resuscitation.
immediateSteps: Call 108 immediately
Remove the person from water without putting yourself at risk
Check for breathing and consciousness
If not breathing, begin rescue breaths immediately
Start CPR: 30 chest compressions, 2 rescue breaths
Continue CPR until emergency services arrive
Place in recovery position if breathing resumes
doList: Call for help immediately
Use a reaching aid (pole, rope) to rescue if possible
Begin CPR immediately if not breathing
Keep the person warm after rescue
Get medical evaluation even if person seems fine
dontList: Do NOT jump in if you are not a strong swimmer
Do NOT try to drain water from lungs
Do NOT give up CPR too soon
Do NOT leave the person alone after rescue
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 immediately for any drowning incident. Even near-drowning needs medical evaluation.
preventionTips: Supervise children near water at all times

categories.name: மின் அதிர்ச்சி
categories.description: Electrical injuries can cause internal damage not visible externally. Immediate medical care is essential.
immediateSteps: Do NOT touch the person if they are still in contact with the source
Turn off the power source if safely possible
Call 108 immediately
Use a dry, non-conductive object to separate person from source
Check for breathing and pulse
Begin CPR if the person is not breathing
Cover any burns with sterile bandage
doList: Ensure the power source is off before touching the person
Call emergency services immediately
Begin CPR if needed
Treat any visible burns
Monitor for cardiac abnormalities
dontList: Do NOT touch someone still connected to electrical source
Do NOT use wet objects to separate from source
Do NOT move the person if spine injury is suspected
Do NOT underestimate internal injuries
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 for ALL electrical injuries. Internal damage may not be visible.
preventionTips: Keep electrical appliances away from water
Do not use damaged electrical cords

categories.name: விஷம்
categories.description: Poisoning requires specific treatment depending on the substance. Quick identification is crucial.
immediateSteps: Call 108 or poison control immediately
Identify the substance if possible - keep the container
If conscious, do not induce vomiting unless told by medical professional
If poison is on skin, remove contaminated clothing and rinse with water
If poison is inhaled, move to fresh air immediately
If unconscious, place in recovery position
Monitor breathing and be ready to perform CPR
doList: Call poison control or 108 immediately
Keep the substance container for identification
Note the time and amount of exposure
Rinse skin or eyes if exposed to chemicals
Keep the person calm
dontList: Do NOT induce vomiting unless directed by medical professional
Do NOT give anything by mouth to an unconscious person
Do NOT wait for symptoms to appear before calling for help
Do NOT neutralize poison with another substance
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 immediately for any suspected poisoning.
preventionTips: Store chemicals and medicines out of children's reach
Keep products in original labeled containers
Read labels before using any chemical product

categories.name: Fainting / Unconsciousness
categories.description: Fainting is a temporary loss of consciousness due to reduced blood flow to the brain.
immediateSteps: Catch the person or guide them safely to the ground
Lay them flat on their back
Elevate their legs about 12 inches above heart level
Loosen any tight clothing, belts, or collars
Check for breathing and pulse
If not breathing, call 108 and begin CPR
When they regain consciousness, have them stay lying down for 10 minutes
doList: Lay the person flat and elevate legs
Ensure adequate air circulation
Offer water once fully conscious
Check for injuries from the fall
Monitor for repeated episodes
dontList: Do NOT splash water on the face
Do NOT slap the person
Do NOT give food or water while unconscious
Do NOT prop them up in a sitting position
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 if the person does not regain consciousness within 1 minute, or if they have chest pain, difficulty breathing, or seizures.
preventionTips: Get up slowly from lying or sitting position

categories.name: Allergic Reaction
categories.description: Severe allergic reactions (anaphylaxis) can be fatal within minutes without treatment.
immediateSteps: Call 108 immediately for severe reactions
Use an epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) if available
Inject into outer thigh, even through clothing
Have the person lie down with legs elevated
If breathing is difficult, allow them to sit up
Loosen tight clothing
Be prepared to perform CPR if needed
doList: Administer epinephrine immediately if available
Call emergency services
Note the allergen that caused the reaction
Monitor breathing and consciousness
A second dose of epinephrine may be given after 5-15 minutes
dontList: Do NOT wait to see if symptoms improve on their own
Do NOT have the person stand or walk
Do NOT give oral medications if swallowing is difficult
Do NOT assume antihistamines alone will stop anaphylaxis
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 immediately for any signs of anaphylaxis: difficulty breathing, swelling of throat/tongue, rapid pulse drop.
preventionTips: Carry an epinephrine auto-injector if prescribed

categories.name: Seizures
categories.description: Seizures involve uncontrolled electrical activity in the brain causing convulsions.
immediateSteps: Clear the area of hard or sharp objects
Cushion their head with something soft
Time the seizure - note when it started
Do NOT restrain the person or put anything in their mouth
Turn them on their side (recovery position) after convulsions stop
Stay with them until fully recovered
Call 108 if seizure lasts more than 5 minutes
doList: Protect from injury by clearing surroundings
Time the seizure
Place in recovery position after convulsions
Stay calm and reassure them when they recover
Note what happened for medical professionals
dontList: Do NOT restrain the person
Do NOT put anything in their mouth
Do NOT try to hold them down
Do NOT give food or water until fully alert
Do NOT panic
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 if seizure lasts over 5 minutes, if it is the first seizure, if the person is injured, pregnant, or does not regain consciousness.

categories.name: Heat Stroke
categories.description: Heat stroke is a medical emergency where body temperature exceeds 104°F (40°C).
immediateSteps: Call 108 immediately - heat stroke is a medical emergency
Move the person to a cool, shaded area
Remove excess clothing
Cool the person rapidly: apply ice packs to neck, armpits, groin
Fan the person while misting with cool water
If conscious, give small sips of cool water
Monitor temperature and consciousness
doList: Move to cool environment immediately
Cool the body as quickly as possible
Apply cold compresses to pulse points
Give cool water if conscious
Monitor body temperature
dontList: Do NOT give aspirin or acetaminophen for heat stroke fever
Do NOT give fluids if unconscious
Do NOT use alcohol rubs to cool the body
Do NOT ignore early warning signs like cramping or exhaustion
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 immediately. Heat stroke can cause organ damage and death.
preventionTips: Avoid outdoor activity during peak heat hours
Never leave anyone in a parked car
Take breaks in shade during physical activity

categories.name: Dog Bite
categories.description: Dog bites can cause infection and rabies. Proper wound care and medical follow-up are essential.
immediateSteps: Move to safety away from the animal
Wash the wound thoroughly with soap and running water for 10-15 minutes
Apply an antiseptic solution like povidone-iodine
Control bleeding with clean cloth and gentle pressure
Cover with a sterile bandage
Note details about the dog (owned/stray, vaccinated status)
Visit hospital for anti-rabies treatment within 24 hours
doList: Wash wound extensively with soap and water
Apply antiseptic
Get anti-rabies vaccination as prescribed
Complete the full course of vaccination
Report stray dog bites to local authorities
dontList: Do NOT suture the wound immediately (increases rabies risk)
Do NOT apply home remedies like turmeric or chili
Do NOT ignore even minor bites or scratches
Do NOT delay anti-rabies treatment
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Seek medical care immediately for all dog bites. Rabies is 100% fatal once symptoms appear but 100% preventable with timely vaccination.
preventionTips: Do not disturb dogs while eating or sleeping
Teach children how to behave around dogs
Report stray dog issues to local authorities

categories.name: Asthma Attack
categories.description: An asthma attack causes airway narrowing making breathing difficult. Quick treatment prevents escalation.
immediateSteps: Help the person sit upright - do not lay them down
Help them use their rescue inhaler (usually blue) - 1 puff every 30-60 seconds, max 10 puffs
Stay calm and encourage slow, deep breaths
Loosen tight clothing around neck and chest
If no inhaler is available, call 108 immediately
If no improvement after inhaler, call 108
Move away from any known triggers (dust, smoke, allergens)
doList: Help them sit upright
Assist with rescue inhaler
Keep them calm
Remove from trigger environment
Call 108 if inhaler does not help
dontList: Do NOT lay the person down
Do NOT give them cold water
Do NOT panic - it will worsen their anxiety
Do NOT delay using the inhaler
categories.whenToCallEmergency: Call 108 if the inhaler does not provide relief, lips/fingers turn blue, difficulty speaking, or the person is getting worse.
preventionTips: Keep living spaces clean and dust-free
